engineGuide.h2: Land Rover Defender Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Land Rover Defender across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.4 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Ford-sourced Duratorq delivered strong torque, but complex EGR systems quickly defined replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: Defender 90, Defender 90 XS, Defender 110, Defender 110 SE, Defender 110 XS Commercial, Defender 110 County, Defender 130 Station Wagon, Defender 130 Pickup, Defender Hardtop
commonFailures: EGR cooler matrix cracking leading to coolant loss and white smoke (50,000-80,000 miles)
Turbo actuator sticking and wastegate linkage rattle (60,000+ miles)
Coolant pump impeller failure causing overheating under sustained load
entries.cta: Get quotes for 204DTA Land Rover Defender engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Downsized for Euro 5 compliance, interrupted regeneration cycles caused widespread oil dilution in modern Defender sumps.
compatibleVariants: Defender 90, Defender 90 XS, Defender 110, Defender 110 SE, Defender 110 HSE, Defender 110 Commercial, Defender 130 Station Wagon, Defender 130 Pickup, Defender Hardtop
commonFailures: Oil dilution from frequent interrupted DPF regeneration raising sump levels (30,000-50,000 miles)
DPF differential pressure sensor faults triggering limp mode (45,000+ miles)
Injector copper washer leaks causing misfires and excessive exhaust soot
entries.cta: Get quotes for 204DTD Land Rover Defender engine replacement

entries.title: 16L - 2.5 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Robust mechanical layout earned legendary status, though thermal head cracking near injectors required rebuilds.
compatibleVariants: Defender 90, Defender 90 County, Defender 90 Expedition, Defender 110, Defender 110 Station Wagon, Defender 110 County, Defender 130 Station Wagon, Defender 130 Pickup, Defender Cabrio, Defender Hardtop
commonFailures: Cylinder head cracking between injector seats due to thermal cycling (90,000+ miles)
Head gasket breach under sustained high-load operation or towing (70,000+ miles)
Water pump bearing wear leading to coolant leaks and overheating

commonProblems.h2: Land Rover Defender Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Land Rover Defender’s engine reliability varies dramatically across its production run, with failure patterns heavily dictated by generation and duty cycle. Classic workhorses using the Td5 and 300Tdi engines are predominantly affected by cylinder head fatigue, while modern L663 models see elevated search volume for AJ200D timing chain tensioner wear and AJ200P direct injection carbon buildup. Every rebuilt Land Rover Defender engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components engineered to rectify these known weak points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, making the breakdown below a practical decision tool to compare Land Rover Defender engine repair costs against full replacement.

problems.h4: Td5 Cylinder Head Cracking - Why Land Rover Defender Td5 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Land Rover Defender 90/110/130 Td5 (1998-2007, 10P & 15P engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Repeated thermal cycling from heavy towing and sustained off‑road load stresses causes micro‑fractures in the original cast‑iron cylinder head between injector seats, while degraded Viton injector O‑rings allow oil and coolant cross‑contamination.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Head Gasket & Injector Seal Refresh (Preventative/Early Signs)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Labour for head removal, pressure test, new multi-layer steel gasket, and upgraded stainless injector sleeves with revised O‑rings
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if structural head fractures are absent; extends life by 15,000-25,000 miles when combined with corrected torque specifications

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Td5 Land Rover Defender Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a fully reconditioned block with crack‑tested reinforced head, recalibrated injectors, bench‑tested ECU mapping, and 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root thermal stress points; delivers near‑new reliability with corrected injector harness routing and verified compression

problems.recommendation: We recommend an immediate cylinder head pressure test if you spot white exhaust smoke or unexplained coolant loss on a Land Rover Defender Td5. Once hairline cracks breach the water jackets, partial repairs rarely hold under torque, making a fully rebuilt Land Rover Defender Td5 engine the most cost‑effective long‑term solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Td5 engine prices for your Land Rover Defender

problems.group: Land Rover Defender Puma EGR Cooler Failure
problems.h4: Land Rover Defender Puma EGR Cooler Failure - Why Late‑Generation Engines Overheat
problems.affectedModels: Land Rover Defender 90/110/130 Puma (2007-2016, 204DTA & 204DTD engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Internal coolant passages within the exhaust gas recirculation cooler develop stress fractures under sustained highway cruising and stop‑start urban duty, allowing exhaust soot and coolant to contaminate the intake manifold and overload the turbo VNT actuator.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - EGR Cooler Replacement & Turbo Actuator Calibration
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removal of cracked EGR assembly, installation of revised cooler core, carbon clean of intake manifold, and electronic actuator recalibration
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost control and prevents short‑term limp‑mode, but does not address pre‑existing bearing wear on high‑mileage Land Rover Defender Puma turbos

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Puma 2.2L Land Rover Defender Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete supply & fit of a remanufactured unit with upgraded EGR cooler, Garrett turbo refurbishment, revised intercooler pipework, bench‑tested, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates cumulative soot/oil contamination damage across the entire air‑intake and crankcase ventilation system for expedition‑grade reliability

problems.recommendation: On a Land Rover Defender Puma exceeding 70,000 miles with intermittent limp mode or sweet‑smelling coolant loss, we advise against patching the EGR system alone. The soot contamination typically degrades piston ring sealing over time, so investing in a rebuilt Land Rover Defender Puma engine with integrated cooler and turbo upgrades offers far better residual value retention.
problems.cta: Get instant quotes for a rebuilt Land Rover Defender Puma engine

problems.group: Land Rover Defender Ingenium Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Land Rover Defender Ingenium Timing Chain Failure - Why Cold‑Start Rattle Signals Critical Wear
problems.affectedModels: Land Rover Defender 90/110 L663 D200/D240 (2020-2024, AJ200D engine codes)
problems.rootCause: The original hydraulic timing chain tensioner lacks sufficient oil pressure retention during cold starts and short‑journey duty cycles, allowing chain slack that elongates the drive links and compromises cam phaser synchronisation.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Timing Chain & Tensioner Update
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine front cover removal, installation of revised Land Rover‑spec tensioner and reinforced chain rails, phaser recalibration
repairOptions.longevity: Highly effective if performed at the first sign of cold‑start rattle; prevents catastrophic valve/piston interference but leaves existing cylinder wear unaddressed

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt AJ200D Land Rover Defender L663 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a fully strip‑and‑build unit with updated tensioner kit, machined block, new bearings and seals, full 48V MHEV integration calibration, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves chain elongation while resetting internal clearances and addressing secondary wear from delayed oil pressure

problems.recommendation: Do not ignore a Land Rover Defender Ingenium timing chain rattle, even if it disappears after 10 seconds of warm‑up. Once the chain has stretched beyond tolerance or skipped a tooth, minor updates will not restore correct valve timing, and a fully rebuilt AJ200D Land Rover Defender engine with the LTB003‑19 revised tensioner becomes the only reliable fix.
problems.cta: Compare AJ200D engine replacement prices for your Land Rover Defender L663

problems.group: Land Rover Defender Ingenium Carbon Buildup
problems.h4: Land Rover Defender Ingenium Carbon Buildup - Why Petrol P300 Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Land Rover Defender 90/110 L663 P300 (2020-2024, AJ200P engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection fuel delivery bypasses the intake valves, preventing petrol detergents from washing away crankcase vapour oils; over time, hardened carbon deposits restrict airflow, disrupt turbo spool efficiency, and increase combustion chamber temperatures.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Walnut Blast Service & Injector Seal Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Intake manifold removal, media‑blasted valve restoration, installation of updated HPFP injector seals, ECU throttle adaptation reset
repairOptions.longevity: Restores OEM airflow and throttle response; recommended every 40,000 miles as preventative maintenance on direct‑injection Land Rover Defender engines

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt AJ200P Land Rover Defender Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete remanufactured unit supply & fit, pre‑cleaned intake ports, upgraded piston rings and valve stem seals, dyno‑validated, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary when carbon fouling has caused chronic misfires, scored cylinder walls, or high‑pressure fuel pump degradation from contaminated crankcase vapours

problems.recommendation: For a Land Rover Defender P300 exhibiting hesitation under boost or elevated short‑term fuel trims, we always recommend starting with a walnut blast and seal refresh. Only when compression testing confirms cylinder wear or HPFP wear has progressed should you transition to a full Land Rover Defender engine replacement quote.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt P300 engine prices for your Land Rover Defender L663

problems.group: Land Rover Defender 300Tdi Cylinder Head Crack
problems.h4: Land Rover Defender 300Tdi Cylinder Head Crack - Why Classic Diesels Overheat Under Load
problems.rootCause: The original thin‑wall cast head lacks adequate reinforcement around the pre‑chamber injector seats; prolonged low‑speed high‑load off‑roading causes localized overheating that initiates cracks into the coolant galleries.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - MLS Head Gasket Upgrade & System Flush
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Head removal, crack‑pressure testing, installation of multi‑layer steel gasket, upgraded thermostat, and coolant system chemical flush
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable on early‑stage fatigue where pressure tests hold below 1.4 bar; extends head life but does not cure underlying metallurgical stress

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt 300Tdi Land Rover Defender Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a fully reconditioned block with NDT crack‑tested reinforced head, new main/rod bearings, revised water pump, bench‑tested, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Guarantees structural integrity with corrected metallurgy and balanced rotating assembly for modern expedition‑grade reliability

problems.recommendation: Classic 300Tdi Land Rover Defenders are exceptionally resilient, but a head that fails a pressure test should not be reused. We recommend investing in a rebuilt 300Tdi Land Rover Defender engine that includes a crack‑tested reinforced head and MLS gasket, as repeated gasket changes rarely resolve the underlying casting fatigue on high‑mileage units.
problems.cta: Compare 300Tdi engine replacement costs for your Land Rover Defender

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Land Rover Defender
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you are managing classic Td5 injector leaks, late‑Puma EGR degradation, or modern L663 timing chain rattle, engine failure does not have to total your Land Rover Defender. EngineMarket simplifies the rebuild vs replace decision by connecting you directly with vetted UK specialists who quote transparently on fully upgraded Land Rover Defender engines. Every unit is bench‑tested, configured for exact variant compatibility, backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, and delivered nationwide to your preferred fitting centre. Bypass main dealer markups, compare live Land Rover Defender engine prices, and secure a reliable powertrain that matches your vehicle’s true market value.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Land Rover Defender Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Land Rover Defender and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Land Rover Defender Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every engine displacement officially fitted to UK-market Land Rover Defenders is listed below with matching engine codes, generation compatibility, and documented common failure points to ensure accurate identification before purchase.

groups.title: Land Rover Defender Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Land Rover Defender 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced with the 2020 L663 generation, the 2.0-litre Ingenium inline-four diesel serves as the efficient entry point for modern Defender buyers. Available in D200 and higher-output D240 configurations, it integrates a twin-scroll turbocharger and 48V mild-hybrid architecture to optimise low-speed response while maintaining respectable load-carrying capability. This compact unit replaced the older five-cylinder designs, prioritising refined NVH characteristics and stricter Euro 6 emissions compliance without sacrificing the Defender’s utilitarian torque delivery.
commonFailurePoints: Early-production timing chain tensioner wear (Land Rover TSB LTB003-19)
DPF/EGR cooler cracking from frequent short-journey operation.
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover Defender 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Land Rover Defender 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: Debuting in 2011 for the final iteration of the classic L316 chassis, the 2.2-litre Puma diesel replaced the bulkier 2.4-litre predecessor with improved acoustic damping and tighter emissions tolerances. It quickly became the standard workhorse for late-model Defender 90 and 110 fleets, featuring a refined variable-geometry turbo and integrated EGR cooling circuit to meet modern regulatory standards while retaining mechanical field-serviceability.
commonFailurePoints: Garrett turbo actuator sticking caused by excessive soot accumulation in urban duty cycles.
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover Defender 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Land Rover Defender 2.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: Launched during the 2007 model-year refresh, the 2.4-litre Ford Duratorq-derived Puma engine marked the classic Defender’s transition to Euro 4 compliance, necessitating the iconic raised bonnet bulge for ancillary clearance. Designed as a robust torque-monster for agricultural and expedition applications, it utilises a heavy-duty cast-iron block and mechanical fan drive to ensure consistent thermal management in sustained low-gear, high-load scenarios.
commonFailurePoints: Oil pump intermediate drive shaft splines stripping
intercooler hose perishing leading to sudden boost pressure loss.
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover Defender 2.4L diesel engines

items.title: Land Rover Defender 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: Spanning from 1983 to 2007, the 2.5-litre displacement encompasses the legendary 200Tdi, 300Tdi and electronically managed Td5 families that defined the classic Defender’s mechanical philosophy. Evolving from naturally aspirated indirect injection to high-pressure direct injection, this size formed the backbone of the UK commercial and enthusiast markets due to its straightforward architecture, widespread parts availability and proven field-repair resilience.
commonFailurePoints: Cylinder head cracking adjacent to pre-combustion chambers (300Tdi/Td5)
deteriorating injector base O-rings allowing pressurised oil to contaminate valve train components.
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover Defender 2.5L diesel engines

items.title: Land Rover Defender 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: Positioned at the premium end of the L663 lineup, the 3.0-litre Ingenium inline-six diesel pairs a sequential twin-turbo arrangement with a 48V mild-hybrid system to deliver effortless low-RPM response and refined cruising efficiency. Engineered for heavy-towing and expedition-spec variants, it features advanced thermal routing, electronically controlled wastegates and a compact aluminium block that reduces front-axle mass while maintaining structural rigidity under sustained off-road load.
commonFailurePoints: AdBlue injector crystallisation in the exhaust stream
48V DC-DC converter sensor calibration faults under sustained high thermal stress.
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover Defender 3.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Land Rover Defender Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Land Rover Defender 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Making its L663 debut in 2020, the 2.0-litre Ingenium inline-four petrol introduced modern direct injection and fully variable valve lift to the Defender range as a cleaner alternative to traditional diesel architectures. Tuned primarily for urban and regional driving, it combines a lightweight aluminium cylinder block with a twin-scroll turbocharger to deliver progressive mid-range power while preserving the vehicle’s precise handling characteristics and balanced weight distribution.
commonFailurePoints: Direct injection intake valve carbon accumulation requiring periodic walnut-blast cleaning
early high-pressure fuel pump diaphragm degradation.
items.cta: Compare Land Rover Defender 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Land Rover Defender 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Serving as the performance flagship for L663 owners seeking immediate throttle response, the 3.0-litre Ingenium inline-six petrol integrates an electric supercharger with a 48V mild-hybrid system to completely eliminate traditional turbo lag. This sophisticated powertrain bridges everyday refinement with serious off-road capability, featuring intelligent cylinder deactivation and optimised coolant management to sustain high-output performance during prolonged high-load terrain response cycles.
commonFailurePoints: belt-driven starter generator (BSG) voltage regulation faults during aggressive thermal cycling.
items.cta: Compare Land Rover Defender 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Land Rover Defender 5.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced to the L663 platform in 2022, the supercharged 5.0-litre V8 petrol represents the ultimate performance evolution for the Defender, delivering track-capable acceleration while retaining full Terrain Response 2 off-road calibration. Based on Jaguar Land Rover’s proven AJ133 architecture, it features a twin-screw roots-style supercharger, forged rotating assembly and reinforced oil pan to withstand extreme mechanical and thermal stress during high-torque traction demands.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain guide material degradation on heavily utilised
items.cta: Compare Land Rover Defender 5.0L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Land Rover Defender Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers all major Land Rover Defender engine changes from 1990 to present, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine by year and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The 200Tdi replaced the naturally aspirated 2.5L diesel, bringing turbocharging and direct injection to the Defender for the first time.
keyChanges: Introduction of 200Tdi turbocharged direct injection diesel engine
Revised cylinder head design with improved cooling passages
Bosch VE rotary injection pump fitted across range
Intercooler option introduced on 110 and 130 wheelbase models
mainEngines: 13L - 2.5L diesel (Range Rover fitment, Defender swap applications)
knownFor: Robust bottom end with strong low-end torque for off-road use
Head gasket upgrades recommended for high-mileage examples
Bosch VE pump requires specialist calibration for optimal performance
Period-correct rebuilds retain classic character with modern reliability
years.cta: Get quotes for 1990-1994 Land Rover Defender 200Tdi replacement engines
years.ticker: Defender 200Tdi head gasket upgrade • Defender 90 2.5L engine replacement • Defender 200Tdi Bosch pump rebuild

years.description: The 300Tdi brought mechanical refinement, serpentine belt drive and improved emissions to the classic Defender platform.
keyChanges: 300Tdi replaced 200Tdi with revised cylinder head and injection system
Serpentine auxiliary belt system replaced multiple V-belts
Improved NVH with acoustic engine covers on later models
Euro 2 emissions compliance introduced mid-production
mainEngines: 19L - 2.5L diesel (automatic transmission variants with Bosch EDC)
20L - 2.5L diesel (export and right-hand drive tropical spec)
21L - 2.5L diesel (late production Euro 2 with acoustic cover)
knownFor: Cylinder head cracking near injector seats remains key failure point
Multi-layer steel head gaskets now standard in specialist rebuilds
Strong aftermarket support for performance upgrades and restorations
Mechanically simple design favoured by off-road enthusiasts and restorers
years.cta: Get quotes for 1994-1998 Land Rover Defender 300Tdi replacement engines
years.ticker: Defender 300Tdi head crack repair • Defender 110 2.5L engine replacement • Defender 300Tdi serpentine belt conversion

years.preview: Early Td5: Electronic Diesel Management Arrives
years.description: The Td5 introduced electronic engine management to the Defender, improving refinement but adding new failure modes around the injector harness and cylinder head.
keyChanges: Td5 10P engine introduced with electronic unit injectors and ECU control
Revised cylinder head design (later reinforced to address cracking)
Injector wiring harness relocated to reduce oil contamination risk
Enhanced diagnostic capability via OBD-II compatible systems
mainEngines: 16P - 2.5L diesel (military and export spec with 24V electrical)
popularModels: Land Rover Defender 130 Td5 Station Wagon
Land Rover Defender Heritage Edition Td5
knownFor: Cylinder head cracking (LR003014) remains most documented failure
Injector seal leaks (LR015678) cause oil contamination in wiring harness
Stainless steel injector sleeves now standard in quality rebuilds
Electronic management enables better diagnostics but requires specialist calibration
years.cta: Get quotes for 1998-2002 Land Rover Defender Td5 10P replacement engines
years.ticker: Td5 cylinder head crack repair • Defender Td5 injector harness fix • Defender 110 Td5 engine replacement

years.preview: Late Td5: EGR and Emissions Updates
years.description: The revised Td5 15P brought updated EGR systems and Euro 3 compliance, improving emissions but adding complexity to maintenance.
keyChanges: Td5 15P engine code introduced with revised EGR valve and mapping
Euro 3 emissions compliance mandated for UK road registration
Updated injector calibration for improved fuel economy
Revised engine mounts to reduce NVH in cabin
popularModels: Land Rover Defender 130 Station Wagon Td5
knownFor: EGR valve sticking and carbon buildup common on high-mileage examples
Cylinder head reinforcement still recommended for preventative rebuilds
Strong torque delivery makes Td5 favoured for towing and expedition use
Specialist rebuilds include updated EGR components and stainless injector sleeves
years.cta: Get quotes for 2002-2007 Land Rover Defender Td5 15P replacement engines
years.ticker: Td5 EGR valve update • Defender Td5 Euro 3 compliance • Defender 90 Td5 engine replacement

years.description: The Puma Duratorq replaced the Td5 with a modern common-rail diesel, bringing improved refinement but new failure points around the EGR cooler and turbo actuator.
keyChanges: Puma 204DTA 2.2L diesel introduced with common-rail injection
Revised bonnet bulge required for turbo and intercooler clearance
Electronic throttle and advanced ECU mapping for emissions compliance
Garrett turbocharger with electronic wastegate actuator fitted
mainEngines: 2.4L Duratorq - 2.4L diesel (commercial and platform chassis variants)
knownFor: EGR cooler cracking (LR034588) leads to coolant loss and overheating
Turbo wastegate actuator failure causes boost pressure issues
Refurbished EGR coolers and updated actuators standard in quality rebuilds
Common-rail system requires specialist diagnostics and calibration

years.preview: Late Puma: DPF and Oil Dilution Mitigation
years.description: The updated Puma 204DTD added DPF and revised EGR mapping to meet Euro 5, introducing oil dilution concerns on short-journey usage.
keyChanges: Puma 204DTD engine code with DPF and updated EGR cooler design
Euro 5 emissions compliance mandatory for new UK registrations
Revised oil specification and service intervals to address dilution risk
Enhanced diagnostic protocols for DPF regeneration monitoring
popularModels: Land Rover Defender 130 Station Wagon Puma
knownFor: Oil dilution from interrupted DPF regeneration on short journeys
DPF cleaning protocol essential for preventative maintenance
EGR cooler updates per Land Rover TSB reduce failure recurrence
Final year of classic Defender production increases parts demand

years.preview: L663 Launch: Ingenium Engines & MHEV Technology
years.description: The all-new L663 Defender introduced Ingenium AJ200D/AJ200P engines with mild-hybrid technology, bringing modern performance but new timing chain tensioner concerns.
keyChanges: All-new D7x aluminium unibody platform replaces ladder chassis
Ingenium AJ200D 2.0L diesel and AJ200P 2.0L petrol engines introduced
48V mild-hybrid system (MHEV) integrated on D240/D300/P400 variants

keyChanges: Revised timing chain tensioner kits fitted to all new Ingenium engines
Updated MHEV software calibration for improved reliability
508PN 5.0L supercharged V8 introduced for Defender 90/110 V8 editions
448DT 4.4L twin-turbo diesel V8 added to 130 range for commercial markets

AJ300P - 3.0L inline-six petrol MHEV (P400, electric supercharger refresh)

faq.h2: Land Rover Defender Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Land Rover Defender engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Land Rover Defender engine replacement costs range from £1,850 for classic 200Tdi units to £9,500 for L663 V8 petrol engines, supply-only. Rebuilt engines with 12-month warranty typically cost £1,900-£2,500 for Td5/Puma classics and £4,900-£7,500 for modern Ingenium diesels. Prices vary by engine code, damage severity, and supplier location across the UK.
keyPoints: Classic 200Tdi/300Tdi rebuilds start around £1,850 supply-only UK wide
Td5 and Puma 2.4 engines typically cost £1,950-£3,200 rebuilt
L663 Ingenium AJ200D/AJ300D replacements range £4,900-£7,500 supply-only
V8 5.0 supercharged units command £7,800-£9,500 for specialist rebuilds
All prices exclude fitting; supply and fit adds £800-£1,500 typically
items.cta: Compare Land Rover Defender engine replacement quotes from UK specialists now

items.question: What is the most reliable Land Rover Defender engine to buy?
items.answer: The 200Tdi and 300Tdi are the most reliable Land Rover Defender engines mechanically, with simple design and proven longevity exceeding 200,000 miles when properly maintained. For modern L663 Defenders, the AJ300D inline-six diesel demonstrates strong reliability once the revised timing chain tensioner per Land Rover bulletin LTB003-19 has been fitted.
keyPoints: 200Tdi/300Tdi classics offer mechanical simplicity and easy DIY maintenance
Td5 reliability improves significantly with upgraded cylinder head and injector sleeves
Puma 2.2/2.4 requires EGR cooler and turbo actuator attention for longevity
L663 Ingenium diesels need updated tensioner kit to prevent chain failure
Regular oil changes every 10k miles critical for all Defender engines
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Land Rover Defender engine with proven reliability record

items.question: Why do Td5 cylinder heads crack on the Land Rover Defender?
items.answer: Td5 cylinder heads crack on Land Rover Defender models primarily due to thermal stress concentration around the injector seats and corrosion in coolant passages, especially on early 10P codes from 1998-2002. Infrequent coolant changes and predominantly short-journey driving that prevents full warm-up cycles significantly accelerate this known failure pattern.
keyPoints: Cracks typically appear near injector sleeves on 10P and early 15P codes
Multi-layer steel head gaskets reduce recurrence risk during rebuilds
Stainless steel injector sleeves prevent oil leakage into coolant system
Pressure-testing heads during rebuild catches micro-cracks before installation
Updated coolant specification and annual changes extend head lifespan significantly
items.cta: Compare Land Rover Defender Td5 rebuilt engines with upgraded cylinder heads

items.question: Is the Ingenium timing chain problem fixed on L663 Defender?
items.answer: The Ingenium timing chain issue on L663 Land Rover Defender models is addressed by fitting the revised tensioner kit per Land Rover technical bulletin LTB003-19. Early AJ200D engines from 2020-2022 benefit most from this update, which prevents chain rattle and potential catastrophic failure on cold starts.
keyPoints: LTB003-19 bulletin covers revised tensioner and chain rail components for AJ200D/AJ300D
Cold-start rattle or rattling above 2,000rpm indicates tensioner wear requiring inspection
Updated kits cost £400-£800 for parts; full rebuild £4,900-£7,500 supply-only
Regular oil changes with Land Rover-approved spec critical to prevent premature wear
MHEV variants require specialist calibration after timing component replacement
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover Defender L663 timing chain repair or engine replacement

items.question: Should I rebuild or replace my Land Rover Defender engine?
items.answer: Rebuild your Land Rover Defender engine if the block is sound and damage is limited to heads, turbos or injectors; replace if there's bottom-end damage, cracked block or severe oil dilution. Rebuilt units cost £1,850-£7,500 supply-only versus £3,000-£12,000 for low-mileage used engines.
keyPoints: Rebuild costs £1,850-£3,200 for classics, £4,900-£7,500 for L663 Ingenium units
Used engines risk unknown history; rebuilt include warranty and updated components
Bottom-end damage, cracked blocks or severe oil dilution warrant full replacement
Rebuilds allow upgraded components like reinforced heads or revised tensioners
Supply and fit typically adds £800-£1,500 depending on garage location
items.cta: Compare Land Rover Defender rebuild versus replacement quotes from UK specialists

items.question: How do I know which engine code my Land Rover Defender has?
items.answer: Find your Land Rover Defender engine code on the engine identification plate near the injector pump on classics or the ECU bay on L663 models. Common codes include 200Tdi 11L, 300Tdi 16L, Td5 10P/15P, Puma 204DTA/DTD, and Ingenium AJ200D/AJ300D. Your VIN decoder also confirms exact fitment.
keyPoints: Classic Defender plates located on injector pump housing or bulkhead firewall
L663 engine codes visible on ECU cover or via OBD2 diagnostic scan
Td5 codes: 10P (1998-2002), 15P (2002-2007), 16P military/export variants
Puma codes: 204DTA (2007-2011), 204DTD (2012-2016) with DPF
Enter VIN on our tool for instant Land Rover Defender engine code lookup
items.cta: Use our VIN decoder to confirm your Land Rover Defender engine code instantly

items.question: Can a Land Rover Defender Puma EGR failure be prevented?
items.answer: Puma EGR cooler failures on Land Rover Defender models can be significantly mitigated by regular intake carbon cleaning, using Land Rover-approved low-ash engine oil, and avoiding excessive short-journey driving that interrupts DPF regeneration cycles. Refurbished EGR coolers fitted with updated software mapping per Land Rover technical service bulletins substantially reduce recurrence risk during engine replacement.
keyPoints: EGR cooler cracks (LR034588) common on Puma 204DTA/DTD 2.2-2.4L engines
Symptoms include white smoke, coolant loss, and oil dilution from exhaust gases
Refurbished coolers with revised gaskets and updated ECU mapping prevent repeat failures
Annual DPF regeneration cycles and oil changes extend EGR system lifespan
Short-journey urban use accelerates carbon buildup; occasional motorway runs help
items.cta: Find Land Rover Defender Puma engines with refurbished EGR systems included

items.question: What mileage can I expect from a rebuilt Land Rover Defender engine?
items.answer: A professionally rebuilt Land Rover Defender engine typically delivers 80,000-150,000 miles of reliable service when maintained with correct oil specifications and service intervals. Classic 200Tdi and 300Tdi units often exceed 200,000 miles post-rebuild. Updated components like multi-layer steel gaskets and revised timing tensioners significantly improve longevity over original factory specifications.
keyPoints: Rebuilt classics with upgraded heads routinely achieve 150k-200k miles with maintenance
L663 Ingenium rebuilds with updated tensioners target 100k+ miles reliably
Full service history and annual oil changes critical to reaching maximum lifespan
Rebuilds include dyno testing and 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard
Avoid short-journey driving and use correct oil spec to maximise engine life
items.cta: Compare Land Rover Defender rebuilt engines with 12-month unlimited mileage warranty

items.question: Are Land Rover Defender engine replacements MoT and ULEZ compliant?
items.answer: Yes, Land Rover Defender engine replacements supplied through our specialist network are fully MoT and ULEZ compliant when fitted with original-specification emissions components. Every rebuilt unit includes certification documentation confirming DPF, EGR and AdBlue system integrity where applicable, ensuring your Defender passes emissions testing and meets London ULEZ or other clean air zone requirements.
keyPoints: All replacements retain original emissions hardware: DPF, EGR, AdBlue where fitted
Documentation package includes emissions certification for MoT and ULEZ compliance checks
Classic pre-DPF Defenders (pre-2007) are ULEZ-exempt based on registration date
L663 Ingenium engines require AdBlue system calibration post-installation for compliance
Keep all paperwork; MoT testers may request emissions component verification
items.cta: Get Land Rover Defender engine quotes with MoT and ULEZ compliance documentation

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a high-mileage Land Rover Defender?
items.answer: Replacing the engine on a high-mileage Land Rover Defender is often worthwhile if the chassis, transmission and body are sound, as a rebuilt engine costs £1,850-£7,500 versus £15,000-£40,000 for a comparable low-mileage replacement vehicle. Classic Defenders especially retain strong resale value post-engine renewal.
keyPoints: Rebuild costs £1,850-£3,200 classics, £4,900-£7,500 L663 versus £15k-£40k replacement vehicle
Classic Defender values often increase £3k-£8k after documented engine renewal
Inspect chassis corrosion, transmission health and body condition before committing to rebuild
Rebuilt engines include 12-month warranty, reducing risk versus buying another used Defender
Supply and fit available nationwide; choose specialist garage familiar with Defender platforms
items.cta: Calculate whether rebuilding or replacing your Land Rover Defender makes financial sense
